title: A phrase I learned by overhearing it
summary: A short reflection about a single phrase you absorbed from neighbours, colleagues, or strangers without studying it.
topic: A phrase I learned by overhearing it
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a foreign phrase you learned not from a book but by hearing it repeated around you. Describe where you kept hearing it, when you finally used it, and what happened. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: For the first month in…, I kept hearing the same short phrase…
I did not look it up. I just listened for…
Then one evening at the bakery, I finally said it…
sampleText: For the first month in my building in Naples, I kept hearing the same short phrase: 'Tutto a posto?' Every morning, while the old man on the second floor was watering his plants in the stairwell, he said it to whoever walked past. Mothers said it to children. The boy at the corner café said it when he handed me my coffee. I did not look the phrase up. I just listened for the situations and guessed. At some point I understood it meant something like 'Everything okay?' — half greeting, half check-in. One Wednesday evening, our lift broke down with me and a small grey-haired woman inside it. She looked nervous. Without thinking, I said, 'Tutto a posto?' She laughed in surprise and said, 'Sì, sì, tutto a posto.' We talked in slow Italian and English for the forty minutes it took for the technician to arrive. When the doors finally opened, she introduced me to her husband as 'il ragazzo del quinto piano.' I had been in Naples for eight weeks, but that was the night I felt I lived there. Some phrases you memorise; others you absorb. The good ones you steal from the air.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I kept hearing the same short phrase
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi cứ nghe đi nghe lại cùng một cụm từ ngắn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: while the old man on the second floor was watering his plants
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: trong khi ông cụ ở tầng hai đang tưới cây

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the boy at the corner café
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cậu trai ở quán cà phê góc phố

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I did not look the phrase up
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi không tra cụm từ đó

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I just listened for the situations and guessed
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi chỉ nghe theo tình huống và đoán
vừa là lời chào, vừa là sự hỏi thăm
thang máy nhà chúng tôi bị hỏng
Không cần suy nghĩ, tôi nói
Bà ấy có vẻ lo lắng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I had been in Naples for eight weeks
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đã ở Naples được tám tuần

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Kept + -ing' for repeated past action
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I kept hearing the same phrase' = nghe đi nghe lại nhiều lần trong quá khứ. Cấu trúc 'keep + V-ing' nhấn mạnh sự lặp lại.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I kept hearing the same phrase' = repeatedly heard it over time. The structure 'keep + -ing' highlights repetition.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect with 'for + duration'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I had been in Naples for eight weeks' dùng past perfect ('had been') với 'for + duration' để chỉ khoảng thời gian dẫn đến một thời điểm khác trong quá khứ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I had been in Naples for eight weeks' uses past perfect ('had been') with 'for + duration' to mark a stretch of time leading up to another past moment.

sampleTranslationVi: Trong tháng đầu tiên ở chung cư của tôi tại Naples, tôi cứ nghe đi nghe lại cùng một cụm từ ngắn: 'Tutto a posto?' Mỗi sáng, trong khi ông cụ ở tầng hai đang tưới cây ở giếng cầu thang, ông nói câu đó với bất kỳ ai đi qua. Các bà mẹ nói với con. Cậu trai ở quán cà phê góc phố nói khi đưa cà phê cho tôi. Tôi không tra cụm từ đó. Tôi chỉ nghe theo tình huống và đoán. Tới một lúc nào đó tôi hiểu nó có nghĩa đại loại như 'Mọi thứ ổn chứ?' — vừa là lời chào, vừa là sự hỏi thăm. Một tối thứ Tư, thang máy nhà chúng tôi bị hỏng khi tôi và một bà cụ tóc bạc đang ở bên trong. Bà có vẻ lo lắng. Không cần suy nghĩ, tôi nói, 'Tutto a posto?' Bà ấy bật cười ngạc nhiên và nói, 'Sì, sì, tutto a posto.' Chúng tôi nói chuyện bằng tiếng Ý và tiếng Anh chậm rãi trong bốn mươi phút thợ kỹ thuật mới đến. Khi cánh cửa cuối cùng mở ra, bà giới thiệu tôi với chồng bà là 'cậu trai tầng năm.' Tôi đã ở Naples được tám tuần, nhưng đó là đêm tôi cảm thấy mình thực sự sống ở đây. Có những cụm từ bạn học thuộc; có những cụm từ bạn hấp thụ. Những cụm hay nhất bạn nhặt từ không khí.

options: I kept hearing the same short phrase.
I kept to hear the same short phrase.
I kept hear the same short phrase.
I have kept hear the same short phrase.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct 'while' + past continuous sentence.
options: While the old man was watering his plants, he said it to whoever walked past.
While the old man watered his plants, he was say it to whoever walked past.
While the old man is watering his plants, he said it to whoever walked past.
While the old man water his plants, he said it to whoever walked past.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was watering' for ongoing background; past simple 'said' for the inserted action.

options: to hear something by accident, not on purpose
to listen very carefully
to repeat a sound exactly
to ignore what someone said
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Overhear' = accidentally hear what is not directly addressed to you.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the natural phrasal verb sentence.
options: I did not look the phrase up.
I did not look up the phrase.
Both A and B are correct.
I did not look the phrase at.
mcQuestions.explanation: Separable phrasal verb 'look up' allows both orders with a noun object.
mcQuestions.hint: Look up = check in a dictionary.

options: It meant something like 'Everything okay?'
It mean something like 'Everything okay?'
It was meaning something like 'Everything okay?'
It have mean something like 'Everything okay?'
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'meant' (irregular) for a finished past realisation.

options: Our lift broke down with me inside it.
Our lift broken down with me inside it.
Our lift was broke down with me inside it.
Our lift breaks down with me inside it.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple of 'break down' = stopped working. Form: broke (past) / broken (past participle).

options: a brief way of asking if someone is okay
the act of arriving at a hotel
a moment for paying a bill
a kind of signature
mcQuestions.explanation: A 'check-in' is a short, caring enquiry into someone's state.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past perfect sentence.
options: I had been in Naples for eight weeks.
I have been in Naples for eight weeks.
I was been in Naples for eight weeks.
I had being in Naples for eight weeks.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had been + for + duration' for a span up to a past moment.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural sentence with 'without thinking'.
options: Without thinking, I said 'Tutto a posto?'
Without to think, I said 'Tutto a posto?'
Without think, I said 'Tutto a posto?'
Without that I think, I said 'Tutto a posto?'
She introduced me to her husband.
She introduced me with her husband.
She introduced me at her husband.
She introduced me by her husband.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Introduce someone to someone' is the fixed collocation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural closing line.
options: The good ones you steal from the air.
The good ones you steal from the air ones.
The good ones is stole from the air.
The good ones you are steal from the air.
mcQuestions.explanation: Inversion 'The good ones you steal' (object first) for emphasis; present simple for a general truth.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Which way of learning a language sticks better — looking words up in a book, or absorbing them from real life? Why?
